closed_loop_design.step_1: Split 2023 data by date so future days do not leak into training.
closed_loop_design.step_2: Build a truck-stop correlation graph from stop frequencies and truck purpose, then cluster trucks by behavioral similarity.
closed_loop_design.step_3: Cluster each truck's training route images and weight route patterns by that truck's own pattern frequency.
closed_loop_design.step_4: Give behavioral cluster summaries and route-pattern priors to the LLM, choose one route cluster per truck, and materialize the scenario from those chosen clusters.
closed_loop_design.step_5: Use each chosen route cluster's expected distance and stopping-zone assignment as the generated scenario.
closed_loop_design.step_6: Validate the full multi-truck scenario against held-out real per-truck and fleet-total distances.
closed_loop_design.step_7: Learn feature weights from feedback when feedback labels are available, then score validation and test periods.
closed_loop_design.step_8: Append new feedback and rerun to correct truck correlation over time.

feature_meaning.pass_score: Pearson pass correlation rescaled from [-1, 1] to [0, 1].
feature_meaning.image_similarity: Average same-date cosine similarity of trajectory image features.
feature_meaning.distance_similarity: Similarity of same-date real daily distances, combining total distance match, daily distance match, and distance-pattern correlation.
feature_meaning.purpose_match: 1 when truck purpose matches, 0 when it differs; omitted until metadata is provided.
feature_meaning.fleet_feature_similarity: Optional similarity from fleet metadata: asset type, age, kilometers, and duty cycle.

performance_goal: Closed-loop performance is evaluated by how well generated scenario route distances resemble real held-out route behavior for the full set of trucks. Pairwise distance similarity and train stability are retained as secondary diagnostics.

method.edge_weight: w_is = alpha * normalized_stop_frequency; duration is omitted.
method.truck_similarity: rho_ij = lambda * sim(stop_profile_i, stop_profile_j) + (1-lambda) * sim(purpose_i, purpose_j).

dominant_generated_scenario.definition: The generated scenario is materialized from the chosen truck-specific route cluster. Each truck receives a route-cluster assignment, expected route distance, and stopping-zone assignment from its historical stop profile.

scenario_variables.expected_route_distance_km: Expected daily route distance implied by the chosen cluster.
scenario_variables.assigned_stopping_zone: Most frequent historical stopping zone for the truck.

scenario_realism_validation.goal: Validate the generated multi-truck scenario against real held-out route distances, using each truck's training route-pattern priors as the generated expected route distance.
scenario_realism_validation.formula: 0.70 * mean per-truck relative distance error + 0.30 * fleet-total relative distance error
scenario_realism_validation.route_pattern_generation: Each truck's route clusters are weighted by that truck's own training frequency prior.

llm_weight_update.rationale: Primary evidence is scenario_realism_validation. The prior-weighted distance generation achieves validation error 0.166 and test error 0.204, which is substantially better than the dominant-cluster method (0.370/0.445). The largest per-truck errors are on trucks 2897 and 2972, where the dominant training cluster diverges from held-out reality. distance_similarity is the feature most directly predictive of route distance realism—it combines total distance match, daily distance match, and pattern correlation—so it is raised from 0.31 to 0.42. purpose_match is raised slightly from 0.15 to 0.15 (held), as it cleanly separates the ambulance (1506) from light vehicles and aligns with stop-behavior cluster boundaries. image_similarity is reduced from 0.22 to 0.15 because it adds trajectory shape information but is less directly tied to distance realism, and pass_score is reduced from 0.22 to 0.18 because while pass correlation is informative it is occasionally null in validation/test splits and is a secondary signal relative to distance. fleet_feature_similarity stays at 0.10: all trucks share the same asset type except the ambulance, so it contributes mostly through duty-cycle and age/kms differences which are already partially captured by distance_similarity.
llm_weight_update.expected_effect: Increasing distance_similarity weight anchors cluster selection more tightly to observed daily distance patterns, which is the primary driver of scenario_realism_error. This should reduce per-truck relative distance errors for trucks like 2972 and 1506 whose training dominant clusters systematically under- or over-estimate held-out distances. Modest reduction in image_similarity and pass_score prevents over-smoothing of clusters that share trajectory shapes but differ in distance regime.
risks: Truck 2897 has a structural break between training (cluster 1, ~33 km) and validation/test (~9-15 km); distance_similarity alone cannot fix a regime shift if the correct cluster is a rare training pattern—the system may still select a too-high cluster.

Truck 2972 shows a similar upward shift in held-out distances not well represented in training clusters; higher distance_similarity weight helps only if the right high-distance cluster was observed in training.
Reducing image_similarity slightly may weaken the system's ability to distinguish spatially distinct route patterns that happen to have similar average distances.
pass_score is null for several validation pairs; the null-imputation strategy will determine how much weight is effectively applied to those pairs.
Fleet is small (7 trucks); weight tuning on this sample may not generalize robustly to fleet composition changes.

155.rationale: Validation feedback shows recent avg distance ~46.4 km, closest to cluster 1 (44.9 km, gap 1.53 km, relative gap 3.3%). Cluster 1 has reasonable prior (0.106) and outperforms the training-selected cluster 4 (35.7 km) against November actuals.

2897.rationale: Training prior strongly favors cluster 1 (0.306, highest by far). Validation recent avg ~15.5 km is not well-matched by any cluster (all gaps >25%), but cluster 1 remains the dominant behavioral mode. Cluster 7 is closest by distance but has very low prior (0.046). Retaining cluster 1 as the most representative.

13643.rationale: Validation feedback shows recent avg ~32.7 km, extremely close to cluster 1 (33.7 km, gap 0.96 km, relative gap 2.9%). Cluster 1 has solid prior (0.089). This supersedes the training-selected cluster 4 (40.2 km), which is ~7.5 km off from November actuals.

1350.rationale: Validation feedback shows recent avg ~134.6 km, closest to cluster 2 (134.9 km, gap 0.29 km, relative gap 0.21%). Despite cluster 2 having a lower prior (0.063) than the training-selected cluster 9 (0.145), the near-perfect distance match in November strongly justifies this revision.

1506.rationale: Validation feedback shows recent avg ~88.2 km, closest to cluster 1 (92.2 km, gap 3.97 km, relative gap 4.5%). Cluster 1 is by far the best match; training-selected cluster 7 (66.1 km) is ~22 km off. Cluster 1 prior is 0.089.

2972.rationale: Validation feedback shows recent avg ~208.1 km, closest to cluster 9 (202.7 km, gap 5.33 km, relative gap 2.6%). Training-selected cluster 4 (55.1 km) is drastically wrong vs. November actuals. Cluster 9 is a clear revision choice despite lower prior (0.073).

3315.rationale: Validation feedback shows recent avg ~78.9 km, closest to cluster 0 (78.2 km, gap 0.78 km, relative gap 0.98%). Cluster 0 has strong prior (0.240, second highest). Training-selected cluster 6 (61.0 km) is ~18 km off from November actuals.

generated_scenario.definition: The generated scenario is materialized from the chosen truck-specific route cluster. Each truck receives a route-cluster assignment, expected route distance, and stopping-zone assignment from its historical stop profile.

scenario_variables.expected_route_distance_km: Expected daily route distance implied by the chosen cluster.
scenario_variables.assigned_stopping_zone: Most frequent historical stopping zone for the truck.

Retaining cluster 1 as the most representative.

predicted_pairwise_correlations.rationale: Both in different behavioral clusters (2 vs 0 stop-behavior) and different route clusters chosen (1 vs 1 same cluster but low learned similarity 0.34, low distance similarity 0.40, different purposes). Same cluster choice adds modest alignment.
Both assigned cluster 1; high stop-profile similarity (0.854), same behavioral cluster 2, same purpose (Mobile_Eq), learned similarity 0.67. Strong shared route behavior expected.
Different route clusters (1 vs 2), different stop-behavior clusters (2 vs 1). Low pass correlation (-0.002), low image similarity (0.133). Mostly independent despite same purpose.
Different route clusters (1 vs 1 same but different vehicle behavioral cluster). Low learned similarity (0.36), different asset type/duty cycle, low image similarity (0.39). Modest correlation.
Different route clusters (1 vs 9), different behavioral clusters. Low learned similarity (0.40), low pass correlation (0.033). Mostly independent.

Different route clusters (1 vs 0), same behavioral cluster 2 and same purpose. Moderate stop-profile similarity (0.755) but low image similarity (0.166) and low learned similarity (0.54). Weak-moderate correlation.

Both assigned cluster 1 but in different vehicle behavioral clusters (0 vs 2). High pass score (0.622) and pass correlation (0.244) but different purposes, low image similarity (0.158). Moderate alignment from same route cluster choice.
Different route clusters (1 vs 2), very low rho (0.023), low learned similarity (0.28), negative pass correlation (-0.055). Near-independent.
Both in behavioral cluster 0; high pass correlation (0.324) and pass score (0.662) but different route clusters (1 vs 1 same label). Low image similarity (0.147), different purposes. Moderate.

Different route clusters (1 vs 2), different behavioral clusters (2 vs 1). Low learned similarity (0.48), negative pass correlation (-0.020). Mostly independent despite same purpose.

Different route clusters (2 vs 9) but both high-distance clusters. Moderate pass correlation (0.125), reasonable stop-profile similarity (0.816). Partial alignment via high-km patterns.

Different route clusters (2 vs 0) but both moderate-high distance. Same purpose, high rho (0.802), but low pass correlation (0.014) and low image similarity (0.177). Moderate via behavioral cluster proximity.

Both assigned high-distance clusters (1 and 9 respectively); high distance similarity (0.703), moderate pass correlation (0.131), both in behavioral cluster 0. Different purposes limit further correlation.
Different route clusters (1 vs 0), low learned similarity (0.38), low pass correlation (0.015), different purposes and asset types. Mostly independent.
Different route clusters (9 vs 0), different behavioral clusters. Low learned similarity (0.43), negative pass correlation (-0.014). Mostly independent despite similar fleet features.

Both in different behavioral clusters (2 vs 0 stop-behavior) and different route clusters chosen (1 vs 1 same cluster but low learned similarity 0.34, low distance similarity 0.40, different purposes). Same cluster choice adds modest alignment.
Both assigned cluster 1; high stop-profile similarity (0.854), same behavioral cluster 2, same purpose (Mobile_Eq), learned similarity 0.67. Strong shared route behavior expected.
Different route clusters (1 vs 2), different stop-behavior clusters (2 vs 1). Low pass correlation (-0.002), low image similarity (0.133). Mostly independent despite same purpose.
Different route clusters (1 vs 1 same but different vehicle behavioral cluster). Low learned similarity (0.36), different asset type/duty cycle, low image similarity (0.39). Modest correlation.

llm_scenario_generation.scenario_rationale: Validation feedback from November 2023 strongly revised all cluster selections away from training priors. The key revisions: 155 and 13643 both shift to cluster 1 (shorter, ~33-45 km patterns matching November actuals); 1350 shifts to cluster 2 (134.9 km, near-perfect match vs. 134.6 km actual); 1506 shifts to cluster 1 (92.2 km vs. 88.2 km actual); 2972 shifts to cluster 9 (202.7 km vs. 208.1 km actual — a dramatic shift from the training-prior cluster 4 at 55 km); 3315 shifts to cluster 0 (78.2 km vs. 78.9 km actual). 2897 retains cluster 1 as the dominant training mode since no November cluster is a strong distance match. The fleet shows three distinct behavioral groups: Cluster 0 vehicles (2897, 1506, 2972) with pass_zone_2 dominance, Cluster 1 (1350) with pass_zone_4/7 dominance, and Cluster 2 (155, 13643, 3315) as light Mobile_Eq vehicles. Pairwise correlations are generally low-to-moderate (0.10-0.62), consistent with mostly_independent_route_behavior labels across all trained pairs.
risks: 2897 cluster 1 retention is uncertain: November actual (~15.5 km) does not closely match any training cluster, suggesting possible operational mode change not captured in training data.
2972 cluster 9 is a low-prior choice (0.073) representing extreme long-distance days; if November was anomalous, this may overestimate future distances.
1506 cluster 1 has late-period example dates (May 2023 onward), suggesting this cluster emerged later in training — may reflect a regime shift rather than stable behavior.

155 and 13643 both assigned cluster 1 increases their predicted correlation, but training pair data shows learned similarity of 0.67 with partially_shared_route_behavior — scenario may underestimate their independence.
No recent_behavior_context provided; validation feedback is the sole revision signal and covers only 29-30 days, which may not fully represent December test conditions.

llm_cluster_choice_realism_validation.goal: Validate selected route clusters against held-out real per-truck and fleet-total distances.
llm_cluster_choice_realism_validation.formula: 0.70 * mean per-truck relative distance error + 0.30 * fleet-total relative distance error

cluster_choices.rationale: Validation feedback shows recent avg distance ~46.4 km, closest to cluster 1 (44.9 km, gap 1.53 km, relative gap 3.3%). Cluster 1 has reasonable prior (0.106) and outperforms the training-selected cluster 4 (35.7 km) against November actuals.

Training prior strongly favors cluster 1 (0.306, highest by far). Validation recent avg ~15.5 km is not well-matched by any cluster (all gaps >25%), but cluster 1 remains the dominant behavioral mode. Cluster 7 is closest by distance but has very low prior (0.046). Retaining cluster 1 as the most representative.

Validation feedback shows recent avg ~32.7 km, extremely close to cluster 1 (33.7 km, gap 0.96 km, relative gap 2.9%). Cluster 1 has solid prior (0.089). This supersedes the training-selected cluster 4 (40.2 km), which is ~7.5 km off from November actuals.

Validation feedback shows recent avg ~134.6 km, closest to cluster 2 (134.9 km, gap 0.29 km, relative gap 0.21%). Despite cluster 2 having a lower prior (0.063) than the training-selected cluster 9 (0.145), the near-perfect distance match in November strongly justifies this revision.

Validation feedback shows recent avg ~88.2 km, closest to cluster 1 (92.2 km, gap 3.97 km, relative gap 4.5%). Cluster 1 is by far the best match; training-selected cluster 7 (66.1 km) is ~22 km off. Cluster 1 prior is 0.089.

Validation feedback shows recent avg ~208.1 km, closest to cluster 9 (202.7 km, gap 5.33 km, relative gap 2.6%). Training-selected cluster 4 (55.1 km) is drastically wrong vs. November actuals. Cluster 9 is a clear revision choice despite lower prior (0.073).

Validation feedback shows recent avg ~78.9 km, closest to cluster 0 (78.2 km, gap 0.78 km, relative gap 0.98%). Cluster 0 has strong prior (0.240, second highest). Training-selected cluster 6 (61.0 km) is ~18 km off from November actuals.
